user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object (car) and the claimed damaged area (rear bumper) with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a significantly damaged and dented rear bumper, supporting the customer's claim of a dent in that area. The damage is extensive, including deformation and missing parts of the bumper.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image (img_1) clearly shows a car's front bumper, which is the claimed object and part.
claim_status_justification: While img_1 shows severe damage to a front bumper, img_2 shows a different, undamaged car, creating a strong contradiction regarding the claimed object. Additionally, the damage in img_1 is far more severe than a 'scratch', indicating a claim mismatch in severity and type.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the claimed car windshield with extensive damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence of a large spider-web crack pattern on the windshield, directly supporting the customer's claim of a spreading crack. Although img_2 shows no damage, img_1 sufficiently verifies the claim.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly depicts the claimed object (car side mirror) and the alleged damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a significant crack on the side mirror glass, which directly supports the customer's claim of a damaged side mirror. While the customer mentioned it's not sitting correctly, the visible crack is sufficient damage to support the overall claim.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show a car, which is the claimed object. Image img_1 shows damage to the rear portion of the car.
claim_status_justification: The customer explicitly claimed 'rear bumper' damage. While img_1 shows dents, they are primarily on the rear fender/quarter panel, not the rear bumper itself. Image img_2 shows the actual rear bumper clearly and it appears undamaged. Therefore, the claimed damage to the 'rear bumper' is not supported by the visual evidence.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object, a car.
claim_status_justification: The headlight, which is the claimed damaged part, is not visible in the provided image (img_1). The image shows the side of the car, and the front with the headlight is out of frame or obscured.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_2 clearly shows the claimed object (car door) and the claimed damage (dent).
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly depicts a dent on the car's door panel, which directly supports the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly depicts a car, the claimed object.
claim_status_justification: While the hood area is visible, the image displays overwhelming severe damage and dirt across the entire front of the car, completely obscuring any specific 'scratch' as claimed. It is impossible to verify the claimed scratch amidst the extensive damage and debris.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows a laptop and its screen, which is the claimed object and part.
claim_status_justification: The image provides clear visual evidence of severe crack damage on the laptop screen, aligning with the customer's claim of a crack on the display glass.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows a laptop with damage to the hinge area, and img_2 confirms the object is a laptop.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence of a broken hinge area, matching the customer's description of a broken hinge causing the screen to wobble and not open smoothly. The damage is clearly visible.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object (laptop keyboard) and the visible water damage matching the claim.
claim_status_justification: The image provided clearly displays water on the laptop's keyboard, corroborating the customer's report of a water spill in that specific area.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image 1 shows the full laptop and image 2 provides a clear close-up of the claimed damaged area, which is a dent on the laptop's corner.
claim_status_justification: Image img_2 clearly shows a dent on the laptop's corner, directly supporting the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly displays the claimed laptop screen with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows a laptop with extensive cracks on the screen, verifying damage to the claimed object part. Although the customer described the damage as "shattered," the visual evidence indicates a severe crack, which is still damage to the screen.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object (laptop) and visible damage in the described area.
claim_status_justification: The image displays visible scratches on the laptop body directly next to the trackpad, consistent with the customer's claim of 'physical damage around the trackpad area'.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly depicts the package and prominently displays the damaged corner as described.
claim_status_justification: The image clearly shows the claimed damage (crushed corner) on the claimed object (package).

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: img_1 clearly shows the claimed object (package) and the claimed damage (torn packaging).
claim_status_justification: Image 1 clearly shows severe tearing on the seal and side of the package, supporting the customer's claim of torn packaging. While Image 2 contradicts the claim by showing an undamaged package, Image 1 provides clear evidence, and per rule 8, if at least one image clearly shows damage, the claim is supported. The non_original_image flag for img_2 further suggests its irrelevance to the actual damage.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the claimed object (package) and the asserted damage (water damage/stains).
claim_status_justification: The image provided clearly displays significant water damage, including prominent stains and discoloration, on the side of the package, directly supporting the customer's claim of a 'water damaged' package with a 'wet-looking stain'.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the inside of an open package, and img_2 shows the exterior of a package. The claimed object is clearly visible.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence that the package contains only packing material and no actual product, supporting the claim that the contents are missing.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image is clear and shows a dented can.
claim_status_justification: The customer explicitly claimed a 'crushed shipping box' and clarified 'The outside box' needed review. The provided image shows a dented metal can, which is an internal product's packaging, not the external shipping box. Therefore, the visual evidence contradicts the claimed object.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: At least one image (img_1) clearly shows the claimed object (package) with visible damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 provides clear visual evidence of a torn security seal on the package, which directly supports the customer's claim of a torn-open package. Despite image img_2 showing an intact seal, rule #8 states to support the claim if at least one image clearly shows the claimed damage.
